name: MVVM (Model-View-ViewModel)
description: Separates UI state from business logic using observable view models.
when_to_use: Default architecture for modern Android (Compose/XML) and iOS (SwiftUI/UIKit).
trade_offs: Boilerplate for simple screens; ViewModel can become a god object if not scoped.
implementation_notes: Android: ViewModel + StateFlow/LiveData. iOS: ObservableObject + @Published. KMM: shared ViewModel.

name: MVI (Model-View-Intent)
description: Unidirectional data flow with immutable state and explicit user intents.
when_to_use: When you need predictable state management and time-travel debugging.
trade_offs: Higher learning curve; more boilerplate for simple CRUD screens.
implementation_notes: Android: Orbit MVI or custom reducer. iOS: TCA (The Composable Architecture). KMM: shared state machine.
keywords: mvi intent reducer unidirectional state composable architecture

name: Clean Architecture
description: Concentric layers (domain → data → presentation) with dependency inversion.
when_to_use: When building large apps requiring testability and independent layer evolution.
trade_offs: Over-engineering for small apps; many interface layers add complexity.
implementation_notes: Domain layer (use cases) → Repository interfaces → Data sources. All platforms share the pattern.
keywords: clean architecture layers domain usecase repository

name: Multi-Module / Feature Modules
description: Split app into independent Gradle/Xcode modules per feature for build speed and isolation.
when_to_use: When build times exceed 2 minutes or team has 3+ developers working in parallel.
trade_offs: Module dependency management is complex; shared resources need a core module.
implementation_notes: Android: Gradle modules (`:feature:home`). iOS: SPM or Xcode frameworks. KMM: shared + platform modules.
keywords: module feature gradle spm framework multimodule

name: Repository Pattern
description: Abstraction layer that mediates between domain and data sources (network + cache).
when_to_use: Always use when the app fetches data from network or local database.
trade_offs: Can mask data source errors if not propagated correctly.
implementation_notes: Interface in domain layer; implementation in data layer. Inject via DI (Hilt/Koin/Swinject).
keywords: repository data source cache network abstraction

name: Dependency Injection
description: Provide dependencies externally rather than creating them inside classes.
when_to_use: Always use for testable and modular code; required in Clean Architecture.
trade_offs: Framework overhead (Hilt annotation processing). Manual DI is simpler for small apps.
implementation_notes: Android: Hilt or Koin. iOS: Swinject or manual. KMM: Koin (multiplatform). Flutter: get_it + injectable.
keywords: dependency injection hilt koin swinject getit

name: Coordinator / Router Pattern
description: Centralized navigation logic separate from view controllers or composables.
when_to_use: When navigation logic is complex and should be testable independently.
trade_offs: Adds indirection; can conflict with platform navigation components.
implementation_notes: iOS: Coordinator protocol + UINavigationController. Android: custom Router class. Compose: NavHost wrapper.

name: Offline-First Architecture
description: Local database is the single source of truth; network syncs in background.
when_to_use: When app must work without connectivity (field apps; travel; emerging markets).
trade_offs: Conflict resolution is hard; increases storage requirements.
implementation_notes: Room/SQLDelight as SSoT → WorkManager/BackgroundTasks for sync → conflict resolution strategy.
keywords: offline first local database sync workmanager room sqldelight

name: Reactive Streams Architecture
description: Observe data changes through reactive streams from database to UI.
when_to_use: When UI must reflect real-time data changes without manual refresh.
trade_offs: Stream lifecycle management is critical; unmanaged streams cause leaks.
implementation_notes: Android: Flow/StateFlow. iOS: Combine/AsyncSequence. KMM: kotlinx.coroutines.flow. Flutter: StreamBuilder.
keywords: reactive flow combine stream observe stateflow

name: Kotlin Multiplatform (KMM)
description: Share Kotlin business logic across Android; iOS; desktop; and web.
when_to_use: When you want shared domain/data layers with native UI per platform.
trade_offs: iOS interop requires careful nullability handling; Swift/Kotlin bridging overhead.
implementation_notes: Shared module: domain + data + networking. Platform modules: native UI. expect/actual for platform APIs.
keywords: kotlin multiplatform kmm kmp shared ios android

name: Compose Multiplatform
description: Share Jetpack Compose UI code across Android; iOS; desktop; and web.
when_to_use: When you want maximum code sharing including the UI layer.
trade_offs: iOS support is maturing; some platform-specific UI may still need native code.
implementation_notes: Shared: @Composable UI + ViewModel. Platform: expect/actual for sensors; camera; permissions.
keywords: compose multiplatform cmp ui shared cross platform

name: SwiftUI + Swift Concurrency
description: Modern declarative UI with structured concurrency for iOS/macOS/watchOS.
when_to_use: When building new iOS projects or modernizing existing UIKit codebases.
trade_offs: Not all UIKit features are available; backward compatibility limits (iOS 16+).
implementation_notes: @Observable (iOS 17+) or ObservableObject. async/await for networking. NavigationStack for nav.
keywords: swiftui swift concurrency async await observable ios
